messages.content: 改写 which enabled to transform the nonpolar α-phase with random orientation of dipoles to the polar β-phase with well-aligned dipoles along the nanofiber axis.

This transformation was made possible by enabling the conversion of the nonpolar α-phase, which had dipoles randomly oriented, to the polar β-phase with dipoles aligned along the nanofiber axis with high precision.
改写The positive and negative voltages were set to 22 kV and -2 kV, respectively. Besides, the distance of the needle-to-collector was 15 cm.
The application of positive and negative voltages were respectively adjusted to 22 kV and -2 kV, while keeping the distance between the needle and collector at 15 cm.

改写Cs2InCl5(H2O) has an orthorhombic structure in Pnma (PDF no. 78-1822) space group, in which each In atom is coordinated with five Cl atoms and one water molecule to construct [InCl5H2O]2− octahedral units.

Cs2InCl5(H2O) possesses an orthorhombic structure in the Pnma (PDF no. 78-1822) space group. Within this structure, each In atom is coordinated with five Cl atoms and one water molecule, thereby forming [InCl5H2O]2− octahedral units.
改写Within this structure, each In atom is coordinated with five Cl atoms and one water molecule, thereby forming [InCl5H2O]2− octahedral units.
The [InCl5H2O]2− octahedral units are formed as a result of the coordination between each In atom and five Cl atoms, along with one water molecule, within the said structure.
改写Figure 2b displays the powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of Cs2InCl5(H2O), which match well with the standard PDF card (78-1822), confirming the excellent crystallinity.
The excellent crystallinity of Cs2InCl5(H2O) is confirmed by the powder X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns depicted in Figure 2b, which closely correspond to the standard PDF card (78-1822).

改写Some vibrational peaks in FT-IR spectrum represent different phases of PVDF-HFP (Figure 2g), including nonpolar α-phase (764 and 975 cm-1), polar β-phase (1277 cm-1), and superimposed β- and γ-phase (840 cm-1).

The FT-IR spectrum featured in Figure 2g displays distinctive vibrational peaks that correspond to various phases of PVDF-HFP. Such phases include the nonpolar α-phase (at 764 and 975 cm-1), the polar β-phase (at 1277 cm-1), and a superimposed β- and γ-phase (at 840 cm-1).
改写The electroactive phase content was calculated according to the following equation (1)
Calculation of the electroactive phase content was performed using equation (1) shown below:

改写where, IE and I764 are the absorbencies at 840 and 763 cm-1, respectively; k840 and k764 are the absorption coefficients at the respective wave numbers, whose values are 7.7 × 104 and 6.1 × 104 cm2 mol-1, respectively.

The calculation of electroactive phase content involved the use of equation (1), with IE and I764 representing the absorbencies at 840 and 763 cm-1, respectively. Meanwhile, k840 and k764 denote the absorption coefficients at the corresponding wave numbers, with their respective values being 7.7 × 104 and 6.1 × 104 cm2 mol-1.
改写1.5CIC@HFP exhibits the highest electroactive phase of 81.25%, whereas pure PVDF-HFP can only reach 61.2% electroactive phase (Figure S7).

As observed from Figure S7, 1.5CIC@HFP displays the highest electroactive phase content of 81.25%, which is notably higher compared to the pure PVDF-HFP, which can only achieve up to 61.2% electroactive phase.

改写The selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns of the crystal area in CIC@HFP also showed diffraction spots corresponding to the crystallographic plane of β (110, 200), as show in Figure 3c, confirming the important role of -OH…F dipole in promoting the recrystallization of β-phase in the nanofibers.

The presence of diffraction spots, corresponding to the crystallographic plane of β (110, 200), in the selected area electron diffraction (SAED) patterns of the crystal area in CIC@HFP (refer to Figure 3c) were observed. This observation substantiated the crucial part played by the -OH…F dipole in facilitating the recrystallization of β-phase in the nanofibers.

改写The binding energy of O 1s was shifted from 531.8 eV of 0.5CIC@HFP to 531.2 eV of 3CIC@HFP (Figure 3x). And the binding energy of F 1s was decreased from 687.3 eV of PVDF-HFP to 686.9 eV of 3CIC@HFP (Figure 3x).

In Figure 3x, it can be observed that the binding energy of O 1s shifted from 531.8 eV of 0.5CIC@HFP to 531.2 eV of 3CIC@HFP. Additionally, a decrease in the binding energy of F 1s was noted from 687.3 eV of PVDF-HFP to 686.9 eV of 3CIC@HFP (Figure 3x).
